canto_title: The Interruption of the Souls and the Three Testimonies

scenes.summary: A voice cries out in alarm, noting the strange way the light is behaving and Dante's strange movement.
Dante turns to see the source of the voice, marvelling at the broken light and the sight of the soul.
Virgil rebukes Dante for his wandering thoughts, advising him to remain steady like a firm tower.
Dante humbly responds that he is following, his clothing stained by a color that makes him look unworthy.
A group of souls approaches from across the slope, chanting the 'Miserere'.
Two souls run forward to ask Dante to provide news of their condition to the living.
Virgil instructs the messengers that they may tell others that Dante is a man of true flesh.
The souls pass by with the speed and density of thick clouds or a driverless crowd.
Virgil warns Dante that the crowd is large and pressing, and tells him to keep moving while listening.
The crowd cries out, asking Dante to remember them and bring news of the living to those who died in sin.
Dante promises to do what he can for these noble spirits as he travels through the worlds.
A soul asks Dante to pray for him in Fano, where he died due to a political vendetta.
The first soul recounts how he was driven into a swamp and died as his blood pooled in the earth.
A second soul, the Bonconte, asks for Dante's pity regarding his abandoned memory.
Dante asks how the soul met such a tragic end away from the battle of Campaldino.
The soul describes being wounded by a blade and the spiritual struggle between an angel and a demon over his soul.
The soul explains how the heavy vapors of the earth rose to form a thick, suffocating mist.
The soul recounts how the rain and floodwaters carried his body into the Arno river, which buried him.
A third soul, La Pia, asks to be remembered and recounts how she died in the Maremma.
